name: Green Tomatillo Mole (Hh)
recipeIngredient: 3 oz Hulled raw pumpkin seeds OR
1/4 tsp Cumin seeds or ground cumin
2 Whole cloves OR pinch of ground clove
recipeInstructions:
1. Toast the pumpkin seeds in a dry frying pan, shaking the pan or stirring constantly until they are browned and toasty (for pine nuts) or until they have browned and popped (for pumpkin seeds). Remove from heat and transfer to a blender jar if using pine nuts or to a bowl if using pumpkin seeds. If using pine nuts, blend with 1 cup of stock until smooth, then strain through a medium-mesh strainer. If using pumpkin seeds, pulverize in a spice mill in batches and sift into a bowl. Stir in 1 cup of stock and mix well. Set aside.
2. Simmer tomatillos in water to cover with the chilies (remove stems, seeds, and membranes from chilies) and simmer for 10 to 15 minutes. Drain and place in blender jar. Add onion, garlic, and cilantro. Grind spices in a spice mill and add to blender jar. Add lettuce leaves and salt. Blend until mixture is smooth.
3. Heat oil in heavy-bottomed saucepan or casserole over medium heat. Add nut or seed mixture and cook, stirring constantly, until the mixture thickens (about 5 minutes). Add tomatillo mixture and cook again, stirring for about 5-10 minutes, until mixture is thick. Stir in remaining 2 cups chicken stock, bring to simmer, cover partially, and simmer for 30 minutes. Taste and adjust seasonings as needed. If too thick, stir in a little more chicken stock.
4. Yield: About 3 cups sauce.
Serve with grilled or poached chicken breasts as main course. Use as dip with crudites, topping for nachos or enchiladas. Add lime or lemon juice or vinegar and use as salad dressing. Sauce freezes well.
